claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is assessed for each child individually.

**First question: How many children are you inquiring about for Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit and I'd like to know if I'm eligible. I have one child I'm asking about.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! I understand you have one child you're asking about.

**Could you please tell me your child's age?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child Alex is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. So Alex is 8 years old, which means they're under 16.

**Next question: Are you responsible for Alex? This means Alex either lives with you, or you're contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay towards their upkeep (at least £25.60 per week)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me, but I do contribute towards their upkeep each week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. You mentioned that Alex doesn't live with you but you contribute towards their upkeep.

**How much do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep each week? To qualify, you need to contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay, which is £25.60 per week.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute £30 a week towards Alex's upkeep - I help with things like clothes and school expenses.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. So you're contributing £30 per week, which is above the £25.60 threshold.

**Next question: Does Alex live with someone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for them? For example, do they live with their other parent or another family member?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their other parent, but they're not claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Alex lives with their other parent who is not claiming Child Benefit.

**Important question: Between you and Alex's other parent, who has the main day-to-day responsibility for Alex? This would be the person who makes most of the daily decisions about Alex's care, upbringing, and welfare - even if Alex physically lives with them.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd say their other parent has the main day-to-day responsibility since Alex lives with them full-time. They handle the daily care and make most of the decisions about Alex's routine and upbringing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me think through your situation carefully based on the Child Benefit eligibility rules.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16, so qualifies as a child)
- You contribute £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep (above the £25.60 threshold)
- However, Alex lives with their other parent full-time
- The other parent has the main day-to-day responsibility for Alex

**Here's the key point:** Under Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child. When a child doesn't live with you, you can still qualify if you contribute at least the Child Benefit amount to their upkeep. However, the person who has the **main responsibility** for the child has the priority to claim.

Since Alex's other parent has the main day-to-day responsibility (they live with Alex and make most daily decisions), they would have priority to claim Child Benefit, even if they're not currently claiming it.

**One more important question: Is there any particular reason why the other parent is not claiming Child Benefit for Alex? Understanding this might affect the assessment.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No particular reason really - they just haven't claimed it. They're aware of Child Benefit but they've chosen not to apply for it.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Let me make my final assessment based on all the information you've provided.

Based on the Child Benefit eligibility rules, I need to consider the situation carefully:

**Your situation:**
- Alex is 8 years old (qualifies as a child under 16)
- You contribute £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep (meets the contribution threshold)
- Alex lives full-time with their other parent
- The other parent has main day-to-day responsibility for Alex
- The other parent is not currently claiming Child Benefit

**The eligibility outcome:**

While you meet the contribution requirement, the key issue is that **the other parent has the main day-to-day responsibility for Alex**. Under Child Benefit rules, when a child doesn't live with you, the person with the main responsibility has the priority to claim Child Benefit for that child, even if they choose not to claim it.

Since the other parent has main responsibility (Alex lives with them and they make the daily care decisions), they would have priority for any Child Benefit claim. This means that even though you contribute financially, **you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex** in this situation.

However, if circumstances were to change - for example, if you had main responsibility for Alex, or if Alex came to live with you - then your eligibility could be different. The other parent also retains the right to claim if they change their mind in the future.

Is there anything else about your situation you'd like to discuss, or any questions about this assessment?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I understand. That makes sense - since Alex lives with their other parent and they have the main day-to-day responsibility, they would have priority even though I'm contributing financially. Thank you for explaining that clearly.
